1.1.verse_text: I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of my spiritual master. I offer my respectful obeisances to Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu, who appeared in this world with Nityananda Prabhu and is surrounded by His devotees.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to whom does the author first offer his respectful obeisances before beginning the work?
questions.explanation: The text begins with an invocation of obeisances specifically directed towards the lotus feet of the author's spiritual master.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which two personalities are mentioned as having appeared in this world together with Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu?
questions.explanation: The opening verse explicitly states that Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu appeared in this world along with Nityananda Prabhu and is surrounded by His devotees.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, after honoring his spiritual master and Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu, who is the next divine personality the author offers his obeisances to?
questions.explanation: Following the initial offerings to his spiritual master and Lord Caitanya, the author proceeds to offer his obeisances to the lotus feet of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what unique effect does Lord Nandanandana have upon the residents of Vrndavana?
options: He provides them with abundant milk
questions.explanation: The text describes Lord Nandanandana as the one who enchants the minds of the residents of Vrndavana while being surrounded by the gopis.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what group is described as surrounding Lord Nandanandana?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Lord Nandanandana is surrounded by the gopis as He enchants the minds of the Vrajavasis.

1.2.verse_text: I offer my respectful obeisances to the two lotus feet Srimati Radharani. I offer my respectful obeisances Lord Nandanandana, who is surrounded by the gopis and enchants the minds of the residents of Vrndavana.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to whose lotus feet does the author offer his respectful obeisances at the very beginning of the description of the Divine Couple?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the author offers his respectful obeisances to the two lotus feet of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is Lord Nandanandana described regarding those who surround Him?
options: He is surrounded by the gopis
He is surrounded by the cowherd boys
He is surrounded by the sages
He is surrounded by the forest animals
questions.correct_answer: He is surrounded by the gopis
questions.explanation: The text describes Lord Nandanandana as being surrounded by the gopis as He enchants the residents of Vrndavana.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what effect does Lord Nandanandana have on the minds of the residents of Vrndavana?
questions.explanation: The verse specifically mentions that Lord Nandanandana enchants the minds of the residents of Vrndavana.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which title is used to address the Supreme Lord in the context of being surrounded by the gopis?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the Lord who enchants the gopis as Nandanandana.

1.3.verse_text: The celebrated personal associates of the King and Queen of Vrndavana are briefly but truthfully described with great delight in both Vedic literatures and oral tradition. This book will also describe them.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary motivation for describing the personal associates of the King and Queen of Vrndavana?
options: For the satisfaction of the author's dear friends
To establish a new theological tradition
To win a debate against impersonalists
To document the genealogy of the Vraja-vasis
questions.correct_answer: For the satisfaction of the author's dear friends
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the descriptions are being written for the satisfaction of the author's dear friends, following the path of ecstatic love.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the author mentions that the associates of the Divine Couple are confirmed by which specific sources?
options: Only personal meditation and internal visions
Mathura-mandala residents, devotee-written books, Puranas, and Agamas
Only the Upanisads and the Vedanta-sutra
The songs of the forest birds and the whispers of the Yamuna
questions.correct_answer: Mathura-mandala residents, devotee-written books, Puranas, and Agamas
questions.explanation: The text cites the residents of Mathura-mandala, previous books, Puranas, and Agamas as the foundations for identifying these associates.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what approach is the author taking to describe the personal associates of the King and Queen of Vrndavana?
options: By relying solely on personal creative imagination
By following previous authorities in the path of ecstatic love
By introducing brand new analytical categories
By limiting the study to contemporary historical accounts
questions.correct_answer: By following previous authorities in the path of ecstatic love
questions.explanation: The author specifies that the description follows the previous authorities in the path of ecstatic love of God (prema).

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how does the author describe the nature of the information being presented regarding the associates of the Divine Couple?
options: As a lengthy and exhaustive encyclopedia
As a brief and truthful account
As an entirely speculative theological exploration
As a compilation of secret tantric mantras
questions.correct_answer: As a brief and truthful account
questions.explanation: The text states that the associates are described briefly and truthfully with great delight.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the residents of which region are cited as a valid source for verifying the identities of the Lord's associates?
questions.explanation: The text highlights the residents of Mathura-mandala as one of the key sources of knowledge for the identities of these associates.

1.4-5.verse_text: These associates of the Lord are described by the residents of Mathura-mandala, by the various books written by devotees, various Vedic literatures such as the Puranas and Agamas and by the great devotees and saintly persons. For the satisfaction of my dear friends, these associates of the Lord will now be briefly describing in writing, following the previous authorities in the path of ecstatic love of God.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, by which sources are the personal associates of the Lord traditionally described?
options: By residents of Mathura-mandala, Vedic literatures like the Puranas and Agamas, and saintly devotees
By the forest sages of Dandakaranya, the Upanishads, and the commentary of the four Kumaras
By the inhabitants of Dvarka, the historical chronicles of Bharata-varsha, and the hymns of the Siddhas
By the residents of Vaikuntha, the Brahma-samhita, and the oral traditions of the celestial Gandharvas
questions.correct_answer: By residents of Mathura-mandala, Vedic literatures like the Puranas and Agamas, and saintly devotees
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that these associates are described by the residents of Mathura-mandala, various books by devotees, Vedic literatures such as the Puranas and Agamas, and great saintly persons.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary motivation stated by the author for compiling the descriptions of the Lord's associates?
options: For the satisfaction of his dear friends
To establish his own scholarship amongst the learned panditas of his time
To provide a complete genealogy of the Yadu dynasty for historical records
To refute the arguments of non-devotional philosophers
questions.correct_answer: For the satisfaction of his dear friends
questions.explanation: The author clearly states that he is composing this work for the satisfaction of his dear friends.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the residents of Vrajabhumi are categorized into which three specific groups?
options: Protectors of domestic animals, brahmanas, and others
Cowherd kings, merchant guilds, and the residents of the forest
The gopis, the sakhas, and the elderly cowherd men
Brahmanas, ksatriyas, and those who perform manual labor
questions.correct_answer: Protectors of domestic animals, brahmanas, and others
questions.explanation: The text categorizes the associates of Krsna residing in Vrajabhumi into three divisions: protectors of domestic animals, brahmanas, and others.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what standard does the author follow when describing the associates of the Lord?
options: The path of ecstatic love of God, following previous authorities
The grammatical rules established by the ancient Sanskrit poets
The chronological order found in the historical epics
The system of logical deduction favored by the logicians of Navadvipa
questions.correct_answer: The path of ecstatic love of God, following previous authorities
questions.explanation: The author specifies that he is writing this description following previous authorities in the path of ecstatic love of God.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how does the author describe the nature of his presentation regarding the Lord's associates?
options: Briefly but truthfully with great delight
Extensively and with great academic detail
Poetically but without referencing previous authorities
In great length covering every minute pastime
questions.correct_answer: Briefly but truthfully with great delight
questions.explanation: The text notes that the personal associates of the King and Queen of Vrndavana are described briefly but truthfully with great delight.

1.6.verse_text: The associates of Krsna who reside in Vrajabhumi may be divided into three groups : 1. protectors of domestic animals, 2. brahmanas, 3. others.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the residents of Vrajabhumi who are associates of Krishna are categorized into how many distinct primary groups?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the associates of Krishna residing in Vrajabhumi are divided into three groups.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed as one of the three primary groups of Krishna's associates in Vrajabhumi?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the protectors of domestic animals as the first of the three groups of Vraja associates.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the residents of Vrajabhumi are classified into groups including the protectors of domestic animals, the brahmanas, and which other category?
questions.explanation: The text specifies that the groups are: 1. protectors of domestic animals, 2. brahmanas, and 3. others.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which group is mentioned alongside the 'others' and the 'protectors of domestic animals' among Krishna's Vraja associates?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly lists brahmanas as the second group among the three categories of associates in Vrajabhumi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the total number of divisions for the associates of Krishna who reside in Vrajabhumi is correctly identified as which of the following?
options: Five groups: farmers, traders, priests, royalty, and others
Three groups: protectors of domestic animals, brahmanas, and others
Four groups: gopas, gopis, elders, and infants
Six groups: descendants of Yadu and others
questions.correct_answer: Three groups: protectors of domestic animals, brahmanas, and others
questions.explanation: The text provides a concise classification of Vraja-vasi associates into precisely three groups.

1.7.verse_text: The Protectors of Domestic Animals: These are descendants of the Yadu dynasty and they may be divided into three groups : 1. vaisyas, 2. abhiras, 3. gurjaras.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the protectors of domestic animals in Vrajabhumi are descendants of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies the protectors of domestic animals as descendants of the Yadu dynasty.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, into how many primary groups are the protectors of domestic animals in Vrajabhumi categorized?
questions.explanation: The text states that the protectors of domestic animals are divided into three groups: vaisyas, abhiras, and gurjaras.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed as one of the three groups of the protectors of domestic animals?
questions.explanation: The three groups mentioned are vaisyas, abhiras, and gurjaras.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which group is categorized alongside the vaisyas and abhiras as protectors of domestic animals?
questions.explanation: The text confirms that the protectors of domestic animals consist of vaisyas, abhiras, and gurjaras.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the descendants of the Yadu dynasty living in Vraja who protect domestic animals are known by which three designations?
questions.explanation: The text specifies these three categories as the protectors of domestic animals who descend from the Yadu dynasty.

1.8.verse_text: The Vaisyas: For the most part the most important vaisyas earn their livelihood by protecting cows. Some of the vaisyas are born from lower-caste mothers and they are known as abhiras.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary occupation of the most important vaisyas?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the most important vaisyas earn their livelihood by protecting cows.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, from what specific background are the cowherds known as abhiras said to be born?
options: They are born from lower-caste mothers
They are born from celestial beings
They are born from a union of kshatriyas
They are born from pure brahminical lineage
questions.correct_answer: They are born from lower-caste mothers
questions.explanation: The verse states that some of the vaisyas are born from lower-caste mothers and they are known as abhiras.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the cowherds are classified into which three distinct groups?
questions.explanation: The text classifies the protectors of domestic animals, who are descendants of the Yadu dynasty, into three groups: vaisyas, abhiras, and gurjaras.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to which dynasty are the protectors of domestic animals traced back?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the protectors of domestic animals as descendants of the Yadu dynasty.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides the abhiras and gurjaras, what is the third category of those who protect domestic animals?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly lists vaisyas, abhiras, and gurjaras as the three groups of protectors of domestic animals.

1.9.verse_text: The Abhiras: The abhiras are those who have vaisya fathers and sudra mothers. A little inferior to the vaisyas, who are engaged in cow protection, the abhiras earn their livelihood by protecting buffalo. They are known by various names, such as ghosa.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the parentage of the community of cowherds known as the abhiras?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly defines the abhiras as those who are born from vaisya fathers and sudra mothers.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary occupation of the group of cowherds referred to as the abhiras?
questions.explanation: The verse states that while the vaisyas are engaged in cow protection, the abhiras earn their livelihood specifically by protecting buffalo.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, by what other name are the abhiras commonly known?
questions.explanation: The scripture identifies that the abhiras are known by various names, specifically mentioning ghosa.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the status of the abhiras described in relation to the vaisyas?
options: A little inferior to the vaisyas
questions.correct_answer: A little inferior to the vaisyas
questions.explanation: The text clarifies that because of their birth, the abhiras are considered a little inferior to those vaisyas engaged in cow protection.

1.10.verse_text: The Garjaras: A little inferior to the abhiras, the garjaras earn their livelihood by protecting goats and similar animals. Their bodily features are a little chubby and they reside on the outskirts of Vraja.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which animals are primarily protected by the group known as the Garjaras?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the Garjaras earn their livelihood by protecting goats and similar animals.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific description given for the physical appearance of the Garjaras?
questions.explanation: The verse notes that the bodily features of the Garjaras are described as being a little chubby.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, where do the Garjaras reside in relation to Vraja?
options: Near the banks of the Yamuna
questions.explanation: The text clearly identifies that the Garjaras reside on the outskirts of Vraja.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the social standing of the Garjaras compared to the Abhiras?
options: A little inferior to the Abhiras
questions.correct_answer: A little inferior to the Abhiras
questions.explanation: The text defines the Garjaras as being a little inferior to the Abhiras.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how do the Garjaras differ from the Abhiras in terms of their professional livelihood?
options: The Garjaras protect buffalo while the Abhiras protect goats
The Garjaras protect goats while the Abhiras protect buffalo
The Garjaras protect cows while the Abhiras protect goats
The Garjaras protect buffalo while the Abhiras protect cows
questions.correct_answer: The Garjaras protect goats while the Abhiras protect buffalo
questions.explanation: The Abhiras are described as protecting buffalo, whereas the Garjaras earn their living by protecting goats.

1.11.verse_text: The Brahmanas: Learned in all the Vedas, the brahmanas engage in performing sacrifices, worshiping the Deity and other brahminical occupations.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary expertise for which the brahmanas in Vraja are celebrated?
options: They are learned in all the Vedas
They are experts in trading and crafts
They are skilled in protecting goats
They are masters of cowherding and farming
questions.correct_answer: They are learned in all the Vedas
questions.explanation: The text explicitly defines the brahmanas in Vraja as being learned in all the Vedas.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the specific religious duties performed by the brahmanas living in Vrajabhumi?
options: Tending to the goats and sheep
questions.explanation: The text states that the brahmanas in Vraja engage in the occupations of performing sacrifices and worshiping the Deity.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which five distinct groups of residents in Vrajabhumi are collectively identified as associates of Lord Hari?
options: Vaisyas, abhiras, garjaras, brahmanas, and bahisthas
Gopis, gopas, sakhas, elders, and maidservants
Brahmanas, ksatriyas, vaisyas, sudras, and vanaprasthas
Garjaras, bahisthas, gopas, brahmanas, and sages
questions.correct_answer: Vaisyas, abhiras, garjaras, brahmanas, and bahisthas
questions.explanation: The text provides a summary identifying these five groups as the associates of Lord Hari in Vrajabhumi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the livelihood of the bahisthas mentioned as part of the five groups of associates?
options: Engaging in various trades and crafts
Protecting goats and other similar animals
Herding cows on the outskirts of Vraja
questions.correct_answer: Engaging in various trades and crafts
questions.explanation: The text specifies that the bahisthas earn their livelihood through various trades and crafts.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, where do the garjaras reside in relation to Vraja?
options: Near the banks of the Yamuna
questions.explanation: The text notes that the garjaras, who protect goats, reside on the outskirts of Vraja.

1.12.verse_text: The Bahisthas: The bahisthas earn their livelihood by engaging in various trades and crafts. In this way we have described five kinds of associates (vaisyas, abhiras, garjaras, brahmanas and bahisthas) of Lord Hari in Vrajabhumi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary means of livelihood for the class of associates known as the bahisthas?
options: Engaging in various trades and crafts
Tending to the cows in the pastures of Vraja
Performing Vedic sacrifices for Nanda Maharaja
Herding buffaloes in the forest of Bhandiravana
questions.correct_answer: Engaging in various trades and crafts
questions.explanation: The text explicitly defines the bahisthas as those who earn their living through various trades and crafts.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many categories of Lord Hari's associates in Vrajabhumi are listed when including the bahisthas along with the vaisyas, abhiras, garjaras, and brahmanas?
questions.explanation: The verse concludes by summarizing the five categories of associates: vaisyas, abhiras, garjaras, brahmanas, and bahisthas.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which specific group is mentioned as being distinct from the bahisthas, vaisyas, abhiras, and garjaras in the list of Vraja-bhumi associates?
questions.explanation: The text enumerates five specific groups: vaisyas, abhiras, garjaras, brahmanas, and bahisthas.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the bahisthas are identified as residents who contribute to the economy of Vrajabhumi through which occupation?
questions.explanation: The text clearly identifies trade and craft as the professional scope of the bahisthas within the Vraja community.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is NOT one of the five kinds of Vrajabhumi associates categorized alongside the bahisthas?
questions.explanation: The five categories listed in this specific passage are vaisyas, abhiras, garjaras, brahmanas, and bahisthas; gopi-messengers are mentioned in a different classification context.

1.13.verse_text: Lord Krsna's associates may also be divided into the following eight groups : 1. worshipable superiors, 2. relatives who are on the same level (such as brothers and sisters) 3. gopi messengers, 4. servants, 5. craftsmen, 6. maidservants, 7. contemporary cowherd friends and 8. dear gopi friends. Included among the worshipable superiors are the brothers and similar relatives of Nanda Maharaja, Nanda's contemporary friends, servants, other associates and the elderly gopis. Krsna is always respectful to these superiors.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many distinct groups are Lord Krsna's associates divided into?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly lists the associates of Lord Krsna as being divided into eight specific groups.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which specific category of associates includes the brothers and similar relatives of Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the brothers and relatives of Nanda Maharaja as being included among the worshipable superiors.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how does Lord Krsna behave toward the group defined as worshipable superiors?
options: He is always playful with them
He is always indifferent to them
He is always respectful to them
He is always seeking their guidance
questions.correct_answer: He is always respectful to them
questions.explanation: The text states that Krsna is always respectful to the worshipable superiors.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is explicitly listed as one of the eight groups of Krsna's associates?
questions.explanation: Gopi messengers are explicitly mentioned as the third group in the classification of Krsna's associates.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is identified as a group of associates alongside the craftsmen and maidservants?
questions.explanation: Contemporary cowherd friends are listed as the seventh group of Krsna's associates.

1.14.verse_text: 1. Worshipable Superiors: The worshipable superiors include Krnsa's grandfather, other elderly relatives and also the community of brahmanas.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which specific groups of individuals are classified among Krsna's worshipable superiors?
options: Krsna's grandfather, elderly relatives, and the community of brahmanas
The cowherd boys, the gopi friends, and Nanda Maharaja's brothers
Krsna's younger brothers, the maidservants, and the craftsmen
The messengers, the servants, and the gopis of Vrndavana
questions.correct_answer: Krsna's grandfather, elderly relatives, and the community of brahmanas
questions.explanation: The text explicitly categorizes Krsna's worshipable superiors as including His grandfather, other elderly relatives, and the community of brahmanas.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is Krsna's grandfather Parjanya Maharaja physically described regarding his attire and hair?
options: He has a golden complexion, black hair, and wears yellow garments
He has a fair complexion, white hair, and wears white garments
He has a dark complexion, grey hair, and wears blue garments
He has a reddish complexion, white hair, and wears saffron garments
questions.correct_answer: He has a fair complexion, white hair, and wears white garments
questions.explanation: The text describes Parjanya Maharaja as having a fair complexion, being white-haired, and dressed in white attire.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, why is Krsna's grandfather known by the name Parjanya Maharaja?
options: Because he is like a great cloud showering the nectar of auspiciousness
Because he is the protector of the transcendental cows of Vraja
Because he performed a great sacrifice to please Lord Brahma
Because he is the chief architect of the transcendental Nandisvara-pura
questions.correct_answer: Because he is like a great cloud showering the nectar of auspiciousness
questions.explanation: The text states he is named Parjanya Maharaja because he is likened to a great cloud (parjanya) that showers the nectar of auspiciousness.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, whose advice did Parjanya Maharaja follow when desiring a worthy descendant?
questions.explanation: The text confirms that Parjanya Maharaja followed the advice of Devarsi Narada to engage in the worship of Lord Narayana to receive a worthy descendant.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, where was Parjanya Maharaja worshiping Lord Narayana when he heard the divine voice from the sky?
options: At the banks of the Yamuna
On the peak of Govardhana Hill
questions.explanation: The text states that he was engaged in the worship of Lord Narayana at Nandisvara-pura when the divine voice spoke from the sky.

1.15-16.verse_text: Krsna's fair-complexion, white-haired and white-attired grandfather is named Parjanya Maharaja, because he is like a great cloud (parjanya) showering the nectar of auspiciousness. He is the best of all the residents of Vraja. Desiring a worthy descendant, he followed the advice of Devarsi Narada and engaged in the worship of Lord Narayana, the husband of Laksmi. As he was worshiping Narayana at Nandisvara-pura, a pleasant voice spoke the following words from the sky.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what physical characteristics define Krsna's grandfather, Parjanya Maharaja?
options: A golden complexion, black hair, and yellow attire
A fair complexion, white hair, and white attire
A dark complexion, grey hair, and saffron attire
A reddish complexion, silver hair, and blue attire
questions.correct_answer: A fair complexion, white hair, and white attire
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes Parjanya Maharaja as having a fair complexion, white hair, and wearing white attire.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, for what reason is Krsna's grandfather given the name Parjanya Maharaja?
options: Because he is a great warrior like a thunderstorm
Because he is like a great cloud showering the nectar of auspiciousness
Because he resides in the highest peak of Govardhana
Because he provides shade like a large banyan tree
questions.correct_answer: Because he is like a great cloud showering the nectar of auspiciousness
questions.explanation: The text explains his name Parjanya stems from the comparison to a great cloud that showers the nectar of auspiciousness.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, at which specific location did Parjanya Maharaja perform the worship of Lord Narayana?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Nandisvara-pura as the site where Parjanya Maharaja worshiped Lord Narayana.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, whose advice did Parjanya Maharaja follow before engaging in the worship of Lord Narayana?
questions.explanation: Parjanya Maharaja followed the instructions of Devarsi Narada to seek a worthy descendant through the worship of Lord Narayana.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what was the primary desire that motivated Parjanya Maharaja to worship Lord Narayana?
options: To obtain great wealth for the Vraja community
To defeat the demons of Mathura
To establish a new village in Vraja
questions.explanation: The text states that desiring a worthy descendant, Parjanya Maharaja worshiped the husband of Laksmi, Lord Narayana.

1.17-18.verse_text: "Because of your pious austerities you will obtain five sons. Of these sons the middle one, named Nanda, will be the best. Nanda's glorious son will delight the residents of Vraja. He will defeat all oppositions. Both the demigods and demons will worship Him, touching the jewels of their crowns to His lotus feet."

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many sons was Parjanya Maharaja blessed to receive as a result of his pious austerities?
questions.explanation: The text states that Parjanya Maharaja would obtain five sons due to his worship of Lord Narayana.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of Parjanya Maharaja's sons is identified as the best among them?
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that of the five sons, the middle one named Nanda is the best.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, whose lotus feet will be touched by the jewels of the crowns of both demigods and demons?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that Nanda's glorious son will be worshipped by demigods and demons who touch their crowns to His lotus feet.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the predicted nature of Nanda's glorious son regarding any opposition?
options: He will seek compromise with them
questions.explanation: The text declares that Nanda's glorious son will defeat all oppositions.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who will be delighted by the appearance of Nanda's glorious son?
questions.explanation: The text confirms that Nanda's glorious son will bring delight specifically to the residents of Vraja.

1.19.verse_text: Hearing this, Parjanya Maharaja became happy and decided to reside at the place. He lived there until he saw the Kesi demon arrive and at that time he became frightened and fled with all his associates to Mahavana.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which prominent ancestor of Nanda Maharaja felt joyful upon hearing the prophecy regarding his future sons and decided to reside in that specific place?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that upon hearing the prophecy, Parjanya Maharaja became happy and decided to reside there.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which demon's arrival caused Parjanya Maharaja to feel frightened and abandon his residence?
questions.explanation: The text confirms that Parjanya Maharaja fled the area when he saw the Kesi demon arrive.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, where did Parjanya Maharaja relocate with all his associates after leaving his previous residence in fear?
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that due to his fear of the Kesi demon, Parjanya Maharaja fled specifically to Mahavana.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is Krsna's grandmother, Variyasi, described in terms of her physical stature?
questions.explanation: The text describes Krsna's grandmother, Variyasi, as being short in stature.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the distinct color of the garments worn by Krsna's grandmother, Variyasi?
questions.explanation: The text provides that Variyasi is attired in green garments.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what specific color is attributed to the hair of Krsna's grandmother, Variyasi?
questions.explanation: The text states that her hair is the color of milk.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the complexion of Krsna's grandmother, Variyasi, is likened to the color of which flower?
questions.explanation: The text mentions her complexion is the color of a kusumbha flower.

1.20.verse_text: Krsna's grandmother Variyasi is respected in the land of Vraja. She is short in stature and her complexion is the colour of a kusumbha flower. She is attired in green garments and her hair is the color of milk.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of the hair of Krsna's grandmother, Variyasi?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes Variyasi, Krsna's grandmother, as having hair the color of milk.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific complexion color attributed to Krsna's grandmother, Variyasi?
questions.explanation: The text states that Variyasi's complexion is the color of a kusumbha flower.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what color are the garments worn by Krsna's grandmother, Variyasi?
questions.explanation: The text describes Variyasi as being attired in green garments.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the physical stature of Krsna's grandmother, Variyasi, described?
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Variyasi is short in stature.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, in which region is Krsna's grandmother, Variyasi, held in great respect?
questions.explanation: The text notes that Variyasi is respected specifically in the land of Vraja.

1.21.verse_text: Parjanya Maharaja's brothers are named Urjanya and Rajanya and his sister is an expert dancer named Suverjana. Her husband is named Gunavira and they live in the town named Surya-kunja.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the names of the brothers of Parjanya Maharaja?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies the two brothers of Parjanya Maharaja as Urjanya and Rajanya.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the sister of Parjanya Maharaja who is renowned as an expert dancer?
questions.explanation: Parjanya Maharaja's sister is identified as Suverjana, who is praised for her expertise in dance.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the husband of Suverjana?
questions.explanation: The text states that Suverjana's husband is named Gunavira.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, in which town do Suverjana and her husband Gunavira reside?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names the town of Surya-kunja as the residence of Suverjana and Gunavira.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the expertise of Parjanya Maharaja's sister, Suverjana, described?
options: She is a master of cooking
She is an expert in playing the vina
She is a master of garland making
questions.explanation: The scripture specifically describes Suverjana as an expert dancer.

1.22-23.verse_text: Krsna's father is Nanda Maharaja. Nanda delights the residents of Vraja and he is worshiped by all the worlds. He has a protuberant belly, his complexion is the colour of sandalwood, he is tall in stature and his garments are the colour of a bandhujiva flower. His beard is a mixture of black and white hairs, like rice and toasted sesame seeds mixed together.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is described as the specific complexion of Krsna's father, Nanda Maharaja?
options: The color of a bandhujiva flower
The color of black and white rice
The color of a fresh lotus
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the complexion of Nanda Maharaja is the color of sandalwood.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the garments worn by Nanda Maharaja are compared to the color of which flower?
questions.explanation: The scripture describes Nanda Maharaja's garments as having the color of a bandhujiva flower.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to what are the black and white hairs of Nanda Maharaja's beard compared?
options: White clouds and dark rain clouds
questions.explanation: The text describes Nanda Maharaja's beard as a mixture of black and white hairs resembling rice mixed with toasted sesame seeds.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which physical feature is specifically noted about the midsection of Nanda Maharaja?
options: He has a very slender waist
He has a belly marked with a lotus
He has a belly adorned with jewelry
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that Nanda Maharaja has a protuberant belly as one of his physical characteristics.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the stature of Nanda Maharaja described?
questions.explanation: The text describes Nanda Maharaja as being tall in stature.

1.24.verse_text: Nanda is the king of the cowherd men, the younger brother of Upananda and the intimate friend of Maharaja Vasudeva. Nanda and his wife Yasoda are the king and queen of Vrajabhumi and the parents of Lord Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary familial relationship between Nanda Maharaja and the elder cowherd Upananda?
options: Upananda is the elder brother of Nanda
Upananda is the father of Nanda
Upananda is the younger brother of Nanda
Upananda is the maternal uncle of Nanda
questions.correct_answer: Upananda is the elder brother of Nanda
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that Nanda Maharaja is the younger brother of Upananda.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which exalted personality is identified as the intimate friend of Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes Nanda Maharaja as the intimate friend of Maharaja Vasudeva.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who holds the royal position of Queen of Vrajabhumi alongside Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: The verse states that Nanda and his wife Yasoda are the king and queen of Vrajabhumi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is Nanda Maharaja's role within the community of cowherd men described?
options: As the treasurer of the cowherd men
As the king of the cowherd men
As the priest of the cowherd men
As the protector of the cowherd men
questions.correct_answer: As the king of the cowherd men
questions.explanation: The text introduces Nanda Maharaja specifically as the king of the cowherd men.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, Nanda and Yasoda are celebrated as the parents of which divine personality?
questions.explanation: The verse confirms that Nanda and Yasoda are the parents of Lord Krsna.

1.25.verse_text: Because he possesses the splendor (deva) of great wealth (vasu), Nanda Maharaja is also known as Vasudeva. He is also famous by the name Anakadundubhi and it is known that in his previous birth he was named Drona.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the reason Maharaja Nanda is also addressed by the name Vasudeva?
options: Because he possesses the splendor of great wealth
Because he is the master of all Vraja
Because he is the father of the Supreme Lord
Because he is the king of the cowherd men
questions.correct_answer: Because he possesses the splendor of great wealth
questions.explanation: The text explains that Nanda Maharaja is known as Vasudeva because he possesses the splendor (deva) of great wealth (vasu).

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name famously used to address Maharaja Nanda, besides his primary name and the name Vasudeva?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Nanda Maharaja is famous by the name Anakadundubhi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what was the identity of Nanda Maharaja in his previous birth?
questions.explanation: The text clearly mentions that in his previous birth, Nanda Maharaja was named Drona.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following names is attributed to Nanda Maharaja as a title related to his vast opulence?
questions.explanation: Nanda Maharaja is known as Vasudeva specifically because he possesses the splendor (deva) of great wealth (vasu).

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which two of Nanda Maharaja's names are linked to his specific past qualities or history?
questions.explanation: The text identifies both Vasudeva (based on his wealth) and Anakadundubhi as names by which Maharaja Nanda is famous.

1.26.verse_text: These names of Maharaja Nanda are described in the Mathura-mahima section of the Garuda Purana. Nanda's most intimate friend is named Maharaja Vrsabhanu.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which Purana contains the section titled Mathura-mahima that details the various names of Maharaja Nanda?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that the names of Nanda Maharaja are described in the Mathura-mahima section of the Garuda Purana.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the most intimate friend of Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: The text clearly states that Nanda's most intimate friend is named Maharaja Vrsabhanu.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which section of the Garuda Purana is referenced regarding the descriptions of Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: The text specifically cites the Mathura-mahima section of the Garuda Purana as the source for Nanda Maharaja's names.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, whose intimate friendship with Maharaja Nanda is highlighted as a significant detail of the cowherd society?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Maharaja Vrsabhanu as the intimate friend of Nanda Maharaja.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the names of Nanda Maharaja are provided in a section of which sacred scripture?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the Garuda Purana as the source for the descriptions of Nanda Maharaja's names.

1.27.verse_text: Krnsa's mother is named Yasoda because she bestows (da) fame (yasah) on the cowherd residents of Vraja. She is like the personification of parental love for Krnsa. Her complexion is dark and her garments are like a rainbow.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, why is Krsna’s mother given the name Yasoda?
options: Because she provides shelter to all the cows in Vraja
Because she bestows fame upon the cowherd residents of Vraja
Because she serves the Divine Couple with total devotion
Because she was born in the illustrious lineage of Maharaja Vrsabhanu
questions.correct_answer: Because she bestows fame upon the cowherd residents of Vraja
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states she is named Yasoda because she bestows fame on the cowherd residents of Vraja.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is described as the complexion of Mother Yasoda?
questions.explanation: The text describes Mother Yasoda’s complexion as being dark.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to what is the appearance of Mother Yasoda’s garments compared?
options: The bright petals of a lotus
questions.explanation: The text states that Mother Yasoda's garments are like a rainbow.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what quality is Mother Yasoda described as being the personification of?
options: Pure devotional service to the Lord
Supreme compassion for the residents of Vraja
The beauty of the Vraja forest
questions.explanation: The text notes that Mother Yasoda is like the personification of parental love for Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which specific meaning is derived from the 'yasah' portion of the name Yasoda?
questions.explanation: The text explains the name Yasoda by breaking it down to mean the one who bestows (da) fame (yasah) upon the cowherd residents.

1.28.verse_text: Mother Yasoda's body is of a medium size, neither large nor small. She has long black hair. Her closest friends are Aindavi and Kirtida.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the physical stature of mother Yasoda described?
options: She is tall and slender like a lotus stalk
She is of a medium size, neither large nor small
She is very petite and delicate in frame
She is stout and very tall in appearance
questions.correct_answer: She is of a medium size, neither large nor small
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Mother Yasoda's body is of a medium size, neither large nor small.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the specific physical features mentioned regarding mother Yasoda's hair?
options: It is braided with jasmine flowers
It is short and dark brown
questions.explanation: The text confirms that mother Yasoda possesses long black hair.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are identified as the closest friends of mother Yasoda?
questions.explanation: The verse states that Yasoda's closest friends are Aindavi and Kirtida.

1.29.verse_text: Yasoda is the wife of Nanda, the king of Gokula. She is the queen of the cowherd men in Vraja, the friend of Devaki and the mother of Lord Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which royal figure is identified as the husband of Mother Yasoda?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Yasoda is the wife of Nanda, who is the king of Gokula.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what position does Mother Yasoda hold among the cowherd men of Vraja?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Yasoda as the queen of the cowherd men in Vraja.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is recognized as the mother of the Supreme Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Yasoda as the mother of Lord Krsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which woman is described as being a close friend of Mother Yasoda?
questions.explanation: The text lists Yasoda as being a friend of Devaki.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, over which specific land is Maharaja Nanda the king?
questions.explanation: The text designates Nanda as the king of Gokula.

1.30.verse_text: The friendship of Yasoda is described in the following statement of the Adi Purana : "The wife of Maharaja Nanda was known by two names, Yasoda and Devaki. Partly because they share the same name (Devaki) the wife of Maharaja Nanda and the wife of Maharaja Vasudeva were great friends."

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides Yasoda, what other name is used to identify the beloved wife of Maharaja Nanda?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Maharaja Nanda's wife was known by two names: Yasoda and Devaki.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what specific factor is cited as the primary reason for the deep friendship between the wife of Maharaja Nanda and the wife of Maharaja Vasudeva?
options: They grew up together in the same village
They share the same birth star
questions.explanation: The text mentions that partly because they share the same name (Devaki), the wife of Maharaja Nanda and the wife of Maharaja Vasudeva were great friends.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which ancient scripture is referenced to explain the friendship between Mother Yasoda and Devaki?
questions.explanation: The text attributes the statement regarding the friendship of Yasoda and Devaki to the Adi Purana.

1.31.verse_text: Lord Balarama's mother is named Rohini, because she is filled with a constantly rising (arohini) flood of transcendental bliss. Although she dearly loves her son Balarama, she loves Krsna millions of times more.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, why is Lord Balarama's mother given the name Rohini?
options: Because she is filled with a constantly rising flood of transcendental bliss
Because she was born in the land of Rohini-kunda
Because she is the daughter of the king of the Rohina dynasty
Because she is always adorned with red-colored garments
questions.correct_answer: Because she is filled with a constantly rising flood of transcendental bliss
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states she is named Rohini because she is filled with a constantly rising (arohini) flood of transcendental bliss.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how does the depth of Mother Rohini's affection for Krsna compare to her love for her own son, Balarama?
options: She loves Balarama and Krsna with equal intensity
She loves Krsna millions of times more than Balarama
She loves Balarama slightly more than Krsna
She loves Krsna only as a royal prince of Mathura
questions.correct_answer: She loves Krsna millions of times more than Balarama
questions.explanation: The text clarifies that although Rohini dearly loves her son Balarama, her love for Krsna is millions of times greater.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is described as a defining characteristic of Mother Rohini's inner nature?
options: A constantly rising flood of transcendental bliss
A deep sense of sorrow for her separation from Dvaraka
A strict adherence to the protocols of royal etiquette
A natural inclination toward the life of an ascetic
questions.correct_answer: A constantly rising flood of transcendental bliss
questions.explanation: The verse states her name is derived from the fact that she is filled with a constantly rising flood of transcendental bliss.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, when describing the intensity of Mother Rohini's parental affection, whom does the text identify as the object of her greatest love?
questions.explanation: While Balarama is her biological son, the text notes she loves Krsna millions of times more than she loves Balarama.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the significance of the term 'arohini' in relation to the identity of Mother Rohini?
options: It refers to her residence in the village of Arohini
It describes the constantly rising flood of bliss she possesses
It refers to the lineage of her ancestors
It describes the specific ornaments she wears during festivals
questions.correct_answer: It describes the constantly rising flood of bliss she possesses
questions.explanation: The text links her name to the word 'arohini', signifying a constantly rising flood of transcendental bliss.

1.32.verse_text: Nanda Maharaja's elder brothers are Upananda and Abhinanda and his younger brothers are Sannanda and Nandana. These are the names of Lord Krsna's parental uncles.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the names of the two elder brothers of Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Upananda and Abhinanda are the elder brothers of Nanda Maharaja.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which individuals are identified as the younger brothers of Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Sannanda and Nandana as the younger brothers of Nanda Maharaja.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how are the four brothers of Nanda Maharaja collectively classified in relation to Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The verse concludes by stating that Upananda, Abhinanda, Sannanda, and Nandana are the names of Lord Krsna's parental (paternal) uncles.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is correctly listed as a younger brother of Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: Among the options provided, only Sannanda is identified in the text as one of Nanda Maharaja's younger brothers.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these names belongs to an elder brother of Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: The text lists Upananda and Abhinanda as the elder brothers of Nanda Maharaja.

1.33.verse_text: Upananda's complexion is pink. He has a long beard and wears green garments. Tungi-devi is his dear wife. Both her complexion and garments are the colour of a cataka bird.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific complexion of Nanda Maharaja's elder brother, Upananda?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Upananda's complexion is pink.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what color are the garments worn by the respected uncle Upananda?
questions.explanation: The verse confirms that Upananda wears green garments.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the dear wife of Upananda?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Tungi-devi as the dear wife of Upananda.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what bird is the complexion and the garment color of Tungi-devi compared to?
questions.explanation: The description specifies that Tungi-devi's complexion and garments are the color of a cataka bird.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which physical feature is notably described for Upananda in addition to his pink complexion and green garments?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly mentions that Upananda has a long beard.

1.34.verse_text: Abhinanda wears dark garments and his long beautiful beard is like a great conchshell. Pivari-devi is his wife. She wears blue garments and her complexion is pink.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the unique color of the garments worn by Abhinanda?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Abhinanda wears dark garments.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to what object is the long, beautiful beard of Abhinanda compared?
questions.explanation: The text describes Abhinanda's long, beautiful beard as being like a great conchshell.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the wife of Abhinanda?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Pivari-devi as the wife of Abhinanda.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the complexion of Pivari-devi?
questions.explanation: The text describes Pivari-devi as having a pink complexion.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of the garments worn by Pivari-devi?
questions.explanation: The text states that Pivari-devi wears blue garments.

1.35.verse_text: Sannanda is also known as Sunanda. His complexion is white and he wears dark-colored clothing. He has two or three white hairs and he is very dear to Lord Kesava.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the alternative name by which Sannanda is known?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Sannanda is also known as Sunanda.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the bodily complexion of Sannanda?
questions.explanation: The text describes Sannanda's complexion as white.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of the garments worn by Sannanda?
questions.explanation: Sannanda is described as wearing dark-colored clothing.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what distinctive physical feature does Sannanda possess regarding his hair?
options: A long beard like a conchshell
Curly black hair with gold ornaments
questions.explanation: The text mentions that Sannanda has two or three white hairs.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is Sannanda's relationship with Lord Kesava described?
options: He is very dear to Lord Kesava
He serves as a primary gardener
He is the elder brother of the Lord
He is the custodian of the Lord's jewels
questions.correct_answer: He is very dear to Lord Kesava
questions.explanation: The text notes that Sannanda is very dear to Lord Kesava.

1.36.verse_text: Kuvalaya-devi is Sannanda's wife. Her complexion is the colour of a blue lotus flower and she wears red garments. Nandana wears garments the color of a candata flower and his complexion is like the color of a peacock.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the complexion of Kuvalaya-devi, the wife of Sannanda?
options: The color of a blue lotus flower
The color of a golden champaka
The color of a peacock's neck
The color of a white jasmine flower
questions.correct_answer: The color of a blue lotus flower
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Kuvalaya-devi's complexion is the color of a blue lotus flower.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what color of garments does Kuvalaya-devi wear?
questions.explanation: The text describes Kuvalaya-devi as wearing red garments.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of Nandana's garments?
options: The color of a candata flower
The color of a lotus petal
The color of a monsoon cloud
The color of a rising sun
questions.correct_answer: The color of a candata flower
questions.explanation: The text states that Nandana wears garments the color of a candata flower.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the complexion of Nandana described to be like?

The color of a jasmine bud
