questions.explanation: The text confirms that Ananga-manjari is the younger sister of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who holds the position of Srimati Radharani’s father-in-law?
questions.explanation: The text states that Vrkagopa is the father-in-law of Radharani.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is mentioned as the brother-in-law of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Durmada as the brother-in-law of Srimati Radharani.

2.174.verse_text: Jatila-devi is Radharani's mother-in-law and Abhimanyu is Radharani's so-called husband. Kutila-devi, who is always eager to find fault, is Radharani's sister-in-law.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who serves as the mother-in-law of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly identifies Jatila-devi as the mother-in-law of Radharani.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which individual is identified as the so-called husband of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: Abhimanyu is named as the so-called husband of Radharani in the text.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the defining characteristic of Kutila-devi, Radharani's sister-in-law?
options: She is always eager to find fault
She is the most beautiful among the gopis
She is always engaged in singing the glories of Krishna
She is the eldest daughter of Vrkagopa
questions.correct_answer: She is always eager to find fault
questions.explanation: The text characterizes Kutila-devi specifically as one who is always eager to find fault.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the relation of Kutila-devi to Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text clearly states that Kutila-devi is the sister-in-law of Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, within the family of Radharani's so-called husband, how is Kutila-devi described in relation to her temperament?
options: She is a devoted servant of Radharani
She is the leader of the village girls
questions.explanation: The verse notes her constant eagerness to find fault, establishing her role as a fault-finder.

2.175.verse_text: Lalita, Visakha, Sucitra, Campakalata, Ranga-devi, Sudevi, Tungavidya and Indulekha are the eight dearest friends of Srimati Radharani. These gopis are considered the leaders of all the others.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following gopis is listed as one of the eight dearest friends of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: Lalita is explicitly named as one of the eight principal friends of Radharani in the specified verse.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many gopis are identified as the leaders of all the other gopis in the circle of Srimati Radharani's dearest friends?
questions.explanation: The verse states there are eight dearest friends who are considered the leaders of all others.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which gopi is included among the eight primary friends of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: Indulekha is one of the eight dearest friends (asta-sakhi) mentioned in the verse.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following gopis is counted among the eight dearest friends of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: Tungavidya is identified as one of the eight dearest friends in the text provided.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which gopi is listed alongside Campakalata and Ranga-devi as one of the eight dearest friends of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: Sucitra is named as one of the eight dearest friends alongside Campakalata and Ranga-devi in the verse.

2.176.verse_text: Kurangaksi, Mandali, Mankiundala, Matali, Candralalita, Madhavi, Madanalasa, Manjumedha, Sasikala, Sumadhya, Madhureksana, Kamala, Kamalatika, Gunacuda, Varangada, Madhuri, Candrika, Prema-manjari, Tanumadhyama, Kandarpa-sundari and Manjukesi are among the millions of dear friends (priya-sakhi) of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following gopis is explicitly mentioned as one of the millions of priya-sakhis, or dear friends, of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: Kurangaksi is listed in the verse identifying the various dear friends (priya-sakhi) of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which dear friend (priya-sakhi) of Srimati Radharani is known by the name Manjukesi?
questions.explanation: Manjukesi is specifically named as one of the dear friends of Srimati Radharani in the provided list.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these names belongs to the group of millions of priya-sakhis of Srimati Radharani, as opposed to the jivita-sakhi group?
questions.explanation: Kandarpa-sundari is mentioned among the priya-sakhis, while the others are listed among the jivita-sakhis in the following section.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed as one of the priya-sakhis alongside Prema-manjari?
questions.explanation: Madhavi is listed alongside Prema-manjari as one of the dear friends of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following gopis is identified as a priya-sakhi of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: Gunacuda is included in the list of millions of dear friends of Srimati Radharani mentioned in the text.

2.177.verse_text: Lasika, Kelikandali, Kadambari, Sasimukhi, Candrarekha, Priyamvada, Madonmada, Madhumati, Vasanti, Kalabhasini, Ratnavali, Manimati and Karpuralatika are among those friends (jivita-sakhi) for whom Srimati Radharani is as dear as life.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which specific group of Srimati Radharani's friends consider Her to be as dear as their very own life?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies the listed group of sakhis as jivita-sakhi, those for whom Radharani is as dear as life.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed among the jivita-sakhi of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: Karpuralatika is explicitly named in the verse as one of the jivita-sakhi, whereas Kurangaksi is a priya-sakhi and Kasturi and Manojna are nitya-sakhis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these names belongs to the category of sakhis for whom Srimati Radharani is their life breath?
questions.explanation: Ratnavali is listed in the verse alongside others as a jivita-sakhi, while Manimanjari, Sindura, and Candanavati are listed as nitya-sakhis.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which sakhi is mentioned alongside Kadambari and Sasimukhi as being part of the jivita-sakhi group?
questions.explanation: Candrarekha is named in the list of jivita-sakhi, while Sumadhya, Kamala, and Madhureksana are categorized as priya-sakhis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the relationship defined for the group of sakhis that includes Kalabhasini and Madhumati?
options: They regard Srimati Radharani as dear as their life.
They are the specialized attendants of the morning pastimes.
questions.correct_answer: They regard Srimati Radharani as dear as their life.
questions.explanation: The verse classifies Kalabhasini and Madhumati among those for whom Srimati Radharani is dear as life (jivita-sakhi).

2.178.verse_text: Kasturi, Manojna, Manimanjari, Sindura, Candanavati, Kaumudi and Madira are among Sriamti Radharani's eternal friends (nitya-sakhi).

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed among Srimati Radharani's eternal friends known as nitya-sakhi?
questions.explanation: Kasturi is explicitly listed as one of the nitya-sakhis of Srimati Radharani in the specified verse.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these devotees is identified as one of Srimati Radharani's eternal friends, alongside Manimanjari and Sindura?
questions.explanation: Manojna is one of the seven nitya-sakhis mentioned in this specific verse along with Manimanjari and Sindura.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many eternal friends (nitya-sakhi) of Srimati Radharani are grouped together in the verse mentioning Candanavati?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies exactly seven nitya-sakhis: Kasturi, Manojna, Manimanjari, Sindura, Candanavati, Kaumudi, and Madira.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which friend of Srimati Radharani is mentioned as being among the nitya-sakhis along with Kaumudi and Madira?
questions.explanation: Candanavati, Kaumudi, and Madira are all listed within the same group of eternal friends of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these names signifies an eternal friend (nitya-sakhi) of Srimati Radharani alongside Sindura?
questions.explanation: Kaumudi is included in the specific list of Srimati Radharani's nitya-sakhis provided in the verse.

2.179-181.verse_text: Srimati Radharani's Manjari Friends: Ananga-manjari, Rupa-manjari, Rati-manjari, Lavanga-manjari, Raga-manjari, Rasa-manjari, Vilasa-manjari, Prema-manjari, Mani-manjari, Suvarna-manjari, Kama-manjari, Ratna-manjari, Kasturi-manjari, Gandha-manjari, Netra-manjari, Sripadma-manjari, Lila-manjari and Hema-manjari are among Srimati Radharani's manjari friends. Prema-manjari and Rati-manjari are both also known by the name Bhanumati-devi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which two of Srimati Radharani's manjari friends are also specifically known by the name Bhanumati-devi?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Prema-manjari and Rati-manjari are both also known by the name Bhanumati-devi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed as one of the manjari friends of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: Vilasa-manjari is included in the specific list of Srimati Radharani's manjari friends provided in the text, whereas the others mentioned are categorized as nitya-sakhis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which manjari friend is named alongside Suvarna-manjari in the list of Radharani's eternal associates?
questions.explanation: The text lists Suvarna-manjari followed immediately by Kama-manjari among the manjari friends of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these associates is identified as one of the manjari friends of Srimati Radharani along with Netra-manjari?
questions.explanation: The text lists Gandha-manjari and Netra-manjari sequentially within the group of Srimati Radharani's manjari friends.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which manjari friend follows Sripadma-manjari in the enumeration of Radharani's dear associates?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Sripadma-manjari followed by Lila-manjari and Hema-manjari as manjari friends of Srimati Radharani.

2.182.verse_text: The Objects of Srimati Radharani's Worship: Srimati Radharani's worshipable deity is the sun-god, who enlivens the lotus flowers and acts as an eye for the entire world. Srimati Radharani's maha-mantra is the name of Lord Krsna. Srimati Radharani's benefactor, who brings Her all good fortune, is Bhagavati Paurnamasi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the worshipable deity of Srimati Radharani who enlivens the lotus flowers?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Srimati Radharani's worshipable deity is the sun-god, who acts as an eye for the world.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the maha-mantra of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the name of Lord Krsna as the specific maha-mantra of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is described as the benefactor of Srimati Radharani, bringing Her all good fortune?
questions.explanation: The verse clearly names Bhagavati Paurnamasi as the benefactor who brings all good fortune to Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what role does the sun-god play in relation to the world?
options: He acts as an eye for the entire world
He acts as a provider of rain
He acts as a guardian of the forest
He acts as a chariot driver for Krsna
questions.correct_answer: He acts as an eye for the entire world
questions.explanation: The verse characterizes the sun-god as the one who enlivens lotus flowers and acts as an eye for the entire world.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which specific deity is noted for enlivening lotus flowers?
questions.explanation: The text specifically mentions the sun-god as the one who enlivens the lotus flowers.

2.183.verse_text: Specific Description of Various Gopis: Lalita-devi and the other eight principal gopis, the other gopis and the manjari have forms that are for the most part like the transcendental form of Srimati Radharani, the queen of Vrndavana.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how does the transcendental form of the eight principal gopis, such as Lalita-devi, compare to that of Srimati Radharani?
options: They appear exactly like Lord Krsna
They have forms that are for the most part like the form of Srimati Radharani
They possess forms purely made of effulgent light
They take on the forms of different forest goddesses
questions.correct_answer: They have forms that are for the most part like the form of Srimati Radharani
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Lalita-devi and the other eight principal gopis, as well as the manjaris, have forms that are for the most part like the form of Srimati Radharani, the queen of Vrndavana.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides the eight principal gopis, which other group is mentioned as having a transcendental form similar to the queen of Vrndavana?
options: The elderly gopis of the village
questions.explanation: The text confirms that the manjari group, alongside the eight principal gopis, shares a transcendental form similar to that of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which specific personality is titled as the queen of Vrndavana?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Srimati Radharani as the queen of Vrndavana, whose form serves as the standard of likeness for the principal gopis.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the extent of the resemblance between the principal gopis and Srimati Radharani?
options: They are completely different in every aspect
They are for the most part like Her
They are identical only in their clothing
They are only similar in their service roles
questions.correct_answer: They are for the most part like Her
questions.explanation: The text uses the phrase 'for the most part like the transcendental form' to describe the physical resemblance of the gopis to Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many principal gopis are mentioned alongside Lalita-devi in the description of those who resemble the Queen of Vrndavana?
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Lalita-devi and the other eight principal gopis share this transcendental form likeness with Srimati Radharani.

2.184.verse_text: Vrnda-devi, Kundalat-devi and their followaers assist the divine couple in Their pastimes in the various forests of Vrndavana. Dhanistha-devi, Gunamala-devi and their followers remain in the home of Nanda Maharaja,the cowherd king and assist the Lord's pastimes from there.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which two personalities lead the group that assists the Divine Couple specifically within the forests of Vrndavana?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Vrnda-devi and Kundalata-devi, along with their followers, provide assistance for pastimes in the various forests of Vrndavana.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, where do Dhanistha-devi, Gunamala-devi, and their respective followers primarily station themselves to serve the Lord's pastimes?
options: In the home of Nanda Maharaja
On the banks of the Yamuna
questions.correct_answer: In the home of Nanda Maharaja
questions.explanation: The text confirms that Dhanistha-devi and Gunamala-devi remain in the home of the cowherd king, Nanda Maharaja, to facilitate His pastimes from that location.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary area of service for those gopis who follow Dhanistha-devi and Gunamala-devi?
questions.explanation: The text notes that Dhanistha-devi and Gunamala-devi and their followers are situated at the home of Nanda Maharaja to assist the Lord's pastimes.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is described as assisting the Divine Couple alongside Vrnda-devi in the forest pastimes?
questions.explanation: The verse pairs Vrnda-devi with Kundalata-devi and their followers as those who assist the Divine Couple in the forests of Vrndavana.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which two gopis are specifically identified as remaining in the residence of the cowherd king to assist in the Lord's pastimes?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly names Dhanistha-devi and Gunamala-devi as the individuals who assist the Lord's pastimes from the home of Nanda Maharaja.

2.185.verse_text: Kamada-devi is the daughter of Srimati Radharani's nurse. Kamada is an especially close friend of Radharani. Ragalekha-devi, Kalakeli-devi and Manjula-devi are some of Radharani's maidservants.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, whose daughter is the dear friend of Srimati Radharani named Kamada-devi?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Kamada-devi is the daughter of Srimati Radharani's nurse.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which devotee is identified as being an especially close friend of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Kamada-devi as an especially close friend of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed as one of Srimati Radharani's maidservants?
questions.explanation: Ragalekha-devi is mentioned in the text as one of the maidservants of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who serves alongside Ragalekha-devi and Manjula-devi as a maidservant of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text lists Ragalekha-devi, Kalakeli-devi, and Manjula-devi together as maidservants of Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which specific devotee is explicitly noted for being a maidservant of Radharani along with Ragalekha-devi and Kalakeli-devi?
questions.explanation: The verse lists Manjula-devi alongside Ragalekha-devi and Kalakeli-devi as a maidservant of Radharani.

2.186.verse_text: Nandimukhi-devi and Bindumati-devi are the leaders of those gopis who arrange the rendezvous of Radha and Krsna. Syamala-devi and Mangala-devi are the leaders of those gopis who act as well-wishers of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which two gopis serve as the primary leaders for arranging the secret rendezvous between Sri Sri Radha and Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Nandimukhi-devi and Bindumati-devi are the leaders of those gopis who arrange the rendezvous of Radha and Krsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which two gopis are identified as the leaders of the group of well-wishers dedicated to Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text confirms that Syamala-devi and Mangala-devi are the leaders of those gopis who act as well-wishers of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific devotional service rendered by the gopis led by Nandimukhi-devi and Bindumati-devi?
options: Acting as rivals to Srimati Radharani
Arranging the rendezvous of Radha and Krsna
Acting as well-wishers of Srimati Radharani
questions.correct_answer: Arranging the rendezvous of Radha and Krsna
questions.explanation: The text designates Nandimukhi-devi and Bindumati-devi as leaders of the gopis who arrange the rendezvous of the Divine Couple.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the collective role attributed to the gopis led by Syamala-devi and Mangala-devi?
options: They are leaders of the rivals of Srimati Radharani
They are the gopis who arrange the divine rendezvous
They are the gopis who act as well-wishers of Srimati Radharani
They are the personal maidservants of the Divine Couple
questions.correct_answer: They are the gopis who act as well-wishers of Srimati Radharani
questions.explanation: The scripture identifies Syamala-devi and Mangala-devi specifically as the leaders of the gopis who are well-wishers of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, among the gopis mentioned, who are distinguished as leaders of those who act as well-wishers of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text lists Syamala-devi and Mangala-devi as the leaders of the gopis who are well-wishers of Srimati Radharani.

2.187.verse_text: Candravali-devi is the leader of those gopis who are Srimati Radharani's rivals.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who serves as the leader of the gopis who act as rivals to Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Candravali-devi as the leader of the gopis who are Srimati Radharani's rivals.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which group of gopis is led by Nandimukhi-devi and Bindumati-devi?
options: Those who arrange the rendezvous of Radha and Krsna
Those who act as well-wishers of Srimati Radharani
Those who compete as rivals to Srimati Radharani
Those who are expert musicians composing for Visakha
questions.correct_answer: Those who arrange the rendezvous of Radha and Krsna
questions.explanation: The text states that Nandimukhi-devi and Bindumati-devi are the leaders of the gopis who arrange the confidential meetings of the Divine Couple.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who among the following are identified as the leaders of the gopis who act as well-wishers of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text designates Syamala-devi and Mangala-devi as the leaders of those gopis who specifically act as well-wishers to Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific service performed by the talented musicians Rasollasa, Gunatunga, Kalakanthi, Sukhanti, and Pikakanti?
options: Singing the musical compositions of Visakha
Arranging the secret meetings of Radha and Krsna
Acting as the chief rivals to Srimati Radharani
Serving as the principal well-wishers of Srimati Radharani
questions.correct_answer: Singing the musical compositions of Visakha
questions.explanation: The text confirms that this group of musicians delights Lord Hari by performing the musical compositions of Visakha-devi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which musical artist is mentioned alongside Rasollasa, Gunatunga, Kalakanthi, and Sukhanti as one who delights Lord Hari?
questions.explanation: Pikakanti-devi is explicitly listed as one of the five talented musicians who sing Visakha's compositions to please the Lord.

2.188.verse_text: The talented musicians Rasollasa-devi, Gunatunga-devi,Kalakanthi-devi, Sukhanti-devi, and Pikakanti-devi delight Lord Hari by singing Visakha's musical compositions.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which accomplished musicians delight Lord Hari by performing the musical compositions of Srimati Visakha-devi?
options: Rasollasa-devi, Gunatunga-devi, Kalakanthi-devi, Sukhanti-devi, and Pikakanti-devi
Maniki-devi, Narmada-devi, Kusumapesala-devi, Vilasini-devi, and Rangavati-devi
Tulasi-devi, Malati-devi, Campakalata-devi, Citra-devi, and Indulekha-devi
Ratnamala-devi, Candramukhi-devi, Lalita-devi, Tungavidya-devi, and Sudevi-devi
questions.correct_answer: Rasollasa-devi, Gunatunga-devi, Kalakanthi-devi, Sukhanti-devi, and Pikakanti-devi
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly names these five specific musicians as the ones who sing the compositions created by Visakha-devi to please Lord Hari.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, whose musical compositions are sung by the talented group of gopis headed by Rasollasa-devi?
questions.explanation: The verse states that the musicians delight Lord Hari specifically by singing the musical compositions of Visakha-devi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary service performed by the musical group consisting of Rasollasa, Gunatunga, Kalakanthi, Sukhanti, and Pikakanti?
options: Decorating the forest pathways for the Divine Couple
Offering cooling sandalwood paste to Lord Hari
Delighting Lord Hari by singing compositions
Preparing special flower garlands for Radha and Krishna
questions.correct_answer: Delighting Lord Hari by singing compositions
questions.explanation: The text confirms that these gopis delight Lord Hari specifically through their singing of musical works.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these is one of the specific gopis identified as being among the talented musicians who delight Lord Hari?
questions.explanation: Sukhanti-devi is listed as one of the five gopi musicians in the verse, while the others are listed as instrumentalists in the subsequent context.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, among the group that delights Lord Hari with their singing, which of the following names is explicitly mentioned?
questions.explanation: Gunatunga-devi is explicitly named in the verse as one of the musicians, distinguishing her from those who play instruments in the following passage.

2.189.verse_text: Maniki-devi, Narmada-devi, and Kusumapesala-devi serve the divine couple by playing drums, cymbals, string instruments like the vina, and wind instruments like the flute.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which specific gopi associates serve the divine couple by playing various musical instruments such as drums, cymbals, the vina, and the flute?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Maniki-devi, Narmada-devi, and Kusumapesala-devi as the associates who provide service through their musical skills on drums, cymbals, string instruments, and wind instruments.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the primary musical responsibilities performed by the gopis named Maniki-devi, Narmada-devi, and Kusumapesala-devi?
options: Composing songs, singing, and dancing for the pleasure of the Lord
Playing drums, cymbals, string instruments like the vina, and wind instruments like the flute
Arranging flowers, preparing garlands, and decorating the Divine Couple
Preparing the bed, fanning, and massaging the lotus feet of Radha and Krishna
questions.correct_answer: Playing drums, cymbals, string instruments like the vina, and wind instruments like the flute
questions.explanation: These gopis are specifically identified for their musical service which includes playing drums, cymbals, string instruments (vina), and wind instruments (flute).

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides the vina, what other type of string instrument is mentioned alongside the playing of drums, cymbals, and flutes by the gopis?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions string instruments like the vina as part of the musical service performed by Maniki-devi, Narmada-devi, and Kusumapesala-devi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which instrument is specifically categorized as a wind instrument played by the associates Maniki-devi, Narmada-devi, and Kusumapesala-devi?
questions.explanation: The text categorizes the flute as one of the wind instruments played by these specific gopis in their service to the Divine Couple.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the role of Maniki-devi, Narmada-devi, and Kusumapesala-devi in the pastime of pleasing the Divine Couple?
options: They offer elaborate bhoga and sweets
They engage in the performance of musical arts using drums, cymbals, and various other instruments
They facilitate secret meetings in the kunjas of Vrindavana
They supervise the dressing and ornamentation of Srimati Radharani
questions.correct_answer: They engage in the performance of musical arts using drums, cymbals, and various other instruments
questions.explanation: The text highlights their unique service as musicians who play drums, cymbals, stringed instruments, and flutes to delight the Divine Couple.

2.190.verse_text: In this way we have described some of the sakhis (gopi friends), nitya-sakhis (eternal gopi friends), prana-sakhis (gopi friends who are as dear as life), priya-sakhis (dear gopi friends) and parama-prestha-sakhis (dearest gopi friends)

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which specific musical instruments are mentioned as being played by Maniki-devi, Narmada-devi, and Kusumapesala-devi to serve the Divine Couple?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly lists drums, cymbals, string instruments like the vina, and wind instruments like the flute as the instruments played by these specific devotees.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are the three leaders identified among Srimati Radharani's maidservants?
questions.explanation: The verse clearly states that Ragalekha-devi, Kalakeli-devi, and Bhurida-devi are the leaders of those gopis serving as Srimati Radharani's maidservants.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are the two daughters of Divakirti-devi identified among Srimati Radharani's maidservants?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Sugandha-devi and Nalini-devi as the two daughters of Divakirti-devi serving as maidservants.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, Manjistha-devi and Rangaraga-devi are the daughters of whom?
options: The chariot drivers of the gopas
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Manjistha-devi and Rangaraga-devi are the two daughters of Nanda Maharaja's laundry washers.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the five categories of gopi friends listed as having been described?
options: Sakhis, nitya-sakhis, prana-sakhis, priya-sakhis, and parama-prestha-sakhis
Sakhis, nitya-sakhis, priya-sakhis, krsna-sakhis, and parama-sakhis
Prana-sakhis, priya-sakhis, nitya-sakhis, gopa-sakhis, and prestha-sakhis
Sakhis, madhyama-sakhis, uttama-sakhis, prana-sakhis, and nitya-sakhis
questions.correct_answer: Sakhis, nitya-sakhis, prana-sakhis, priya-sakhis, and parama-prestha-sakhis
questions.explanation: The text lists these five distinct classifications of gopi friends when summarizing the descriptions provided.

2.191.verse_text: Srimati Radharani's Maidservants: Ragalekha-devi, Kalakeli-devi and Bhurida-devi are the leaders fo those gopis who are Srimati Radharani's maidservants. Among these maidservants are Sugandha-devi and Nalini-devi (the two daughters of Divakirti-devi) and Manjistha-devi and Rangaraga-devi (the two daughters of Nanda Maharaja's laundry washers).

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are the three leaders among Srimati Radharani's maidservants?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Ragalekha-devi, Kalakeli-devi, and Bhurida-devi as the leaders of the gopis who serve as Srimati Radharani's maidservants.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are the two daughters of Divakirti-devi mentioned as Srimati Radharani's maidservants?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Sugandha-devi and Nalini-devi as the two daughters of Divakirti-devi who serve as maidservants to Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the occupation of the fathers of Manjistha-devi and Rangaraga-devi?
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Manjistha-devi and Rangaraga-devi are the two daughters of Nanda Maharaja's laundry washers.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which two gopis are described as daughters of the laundry washers of Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Manjistha-devi and Rangaraga-devi as the daughters of Nanda Maharaja's laundry washers.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, whose mother is Divakirti-devi?
questions.explanation: It is stated that Sugandha-devi and Nalini-devi are the two daughters of Divakirti-devi.

2.192.verse_text: Palindri-devi serves Srimati Radharani by dressing and decorating Her. Citrini decorates Radharani with various cosmetics. Mantriki-devi and Tantriki-devi are astrologers who reveal the future to Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which maidservant is specifically engaged in the service of dressing and decorating Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Palindri-devi serves Srimati Radharani by dressing and decorating Her.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific devotional service performed by the gopi known as Citrini?
questions.explanation: The text identifies that Citrini decorates Radharani with various cosmetics.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which two gopis serve Srimati Radharani by acting as astrologers and revealing the future?
questions.explanation: The text states that Mantriki-devi and Tantriki-devi are astrologers who reveal the future to Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which devotee is responsible for the task of dressing the Divine Queen, Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: Palindri-devi is described as the one who serves Srimati Radharani specifically by dressing and decorating Her.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides revealing the future, what is the professional service attributed to Mantriki-devi?
questions.explanation: Mantriki-devi is defined as an astrologer in the text.

2.193.verse_text: Katyayani-devi is the leader of those gopi messengers who are older than Srimati Radharani. Bhagyavati-devi and Punyapunja-devi, the two daughters of Maharaja Nanda's sweeper, are also the maidservants of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who holds the position of the leader among those gopi messengers who are older than Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Katyayani-devi as the leader of the gopi messengers who are senior in age to Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the professional service performed by the father of Bhagyavati-devi and Punyapunja-devi?
options: Gardener for the groves of Vrndavana
Guardsman at the entrance of the palace
questions.explanation: The text identifies Bhagyavati-devi and Punyapunja-devi specifically as the daughters of Maharaja Nanda's sweeper.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, in what capacity do Bhagyavati-devi and Punyapunja-devi serve Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text states that Bhagyavati-devi and Punyapunja-devi serve as the maidservants of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which two gopis are identified as the daughters of Maharaja Nanda's sweeper?
questions.explanation: The text clearly names Bhagyavati-devi and Punyapunja-devi as the two daughters belonging to the family of Maharaja Nanda's sweeper.

2.194.verse_text: Tunga-devi, Malli-devi and Matalli-devi are the leaders of those girls descended from the uncivilized mountain tribe known as the Pulindas. In Vrndavana some of the Pulinda girls act as the friends of Srimati Radharani and some are the friends of Sri Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which three personalities are identified as the leaders of the girls descended from the Pulinda mountain tribe?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Tunga-devi, Malli-devi, and Matalli-devi as the leaders of the girls from the Pulinda tribe in Vrndavana.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to which tribal group do the gopis named Tunga-devi, Malli-devi, and Matalli-devi belong?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies these three leaders as girls descended from the uncivilized mountain tribe known as the Pulindas.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the role of the girls from the Pulinda tribe within the pastimes of Vrndavana?
options: Some act as friends of Srimati Radharani and some as friends of Sri Krsna
They serve exclusively as maidservants in the palace of Nanda Maharaja
They act solely as messengers between the elderly gopis and the Divine Couple
They are tasked only with gathering forest flowers for Krsna's garlands
questions.correct_answer: Some act as friends of Srimati Radharani and some as friends of Sri Krsna
questions.explanation: The text states that in Vrndavana, some of the Pulinda girls act as the friends of Srimati Radharani and some as the friends of Sri Krsna.

2.195.verse_text: Also included among Srimati Radharani's servants are Gargi-devi and other very respectable brahmana girls, Bhrngarika-devi and other girls from the ceti community, Vijaya-devi, Rasala-devi, Payoda-devi and other girls from the vita community, as well as the boys Subala, Ujjvala, Gandharva, Madhumangala and Raktaka.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which respectable group of young women are included among the personal servants of Srimati Radharani alongside those from the ceti and vita communities?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly lists Gargi-devi and other very respectable brahmana girls as servants of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which specific individuals are listed as servants of Srimati Radharani hailing from the vita community?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Vijaya-devi, Rasala-devi, and Payoda-devi as girls belonging to the vita community who serve Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which male associates are mentioned in the same listing of Srimati Radharani's servants alongside the female devotees?
options: Subala, Ujjvala, Gandharva, Madhumangala, and Raktaka
questions.correct_answer: Subala, Ujjvala, Gandharva, Madhumangala, and Raktaka
questions.explanation: The text explicitly lists these five boys as included among the servants of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is mentioned as being from the ceti community among the servants of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Bhrngarika-devi as a girl belonging to the ceti community.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides the vita and ceti communities, which other specific group of young women are mentioned in the context of Srimati Radharani's servants?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions Gargi-devi and other very respectable brahmana girls in the list of Srimati Radharani's servants.

2.196.verse_text: Tunga-devi, Pisangi-devi and Kalakandala-devi always remain near Srimati Radharani to serve Her. Manjual-devi, Bindula-devi, Sandha-devi, Mrdula-devi and others, although very young still engage in Radharani's service.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which three sakhis are specifically described as always remaining near Srimati Radharani to serve Her?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Tunga-devi, Pisangi-devi, and Kalakandala-devi as those who always remain near Srimati Radharani to serve Her.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following groups of sakhis are noted for being very young while still engaging in the service of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Manjula-devi, Bindula-devi, Sandha-devi, Mrdula-devi and others as young servants of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which devotee is listed alongside Manjula-devi and Bindula-devi as being very young yet dedicated to Radharani's service?
questions.explanation: Mrdula-devi is mentioned alongside Manjula-devi, Bindula-devi, and Sandha-devi as young servants.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which companion is mentioned as remaining near Srimati Radharani in the same list as Tunga-devi and Pisangi-devi?
questions.explanation: Tunga-devi, Pisangi-devi, and Kalakandala-devi are grouped together in the text as always remaining near Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, in addition to Manjula-devi, Bindula-devi, and Mrdula-devi, who else is identified as a very young servant of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: Sandha-devi is listed as one of the very young servants alongside Manjula-devi, Bindula-devi, and Mrdula-devi.

2.197.verse_text: Sunada, Yamuna and Bahula are the most important of Srimati Radharani's pet surabhi cows. Tungi is Her chubby pet calf, Kakkhati is Her old pet monkey, Rangini is Her pet doe and Carucnadrika is Her pet cakori bird.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the names of Srimati Radharani's most prominent pet surabhi cows?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Sunada, Yamuna, and Bahula as the most important of Srimati Radharani's pet surabhi cows.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which animal is described as Srimati Radharani's chubby pet calf?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Tungi as the name of Srimati Radharani's chubby pet calf.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the identity of Radharani's old pet monkey?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Kakkhati specifically as Radharani's old pet monkey.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which creature is affectionately kept by Srimati Radharani as Her pet doe?
questions.explanation: The verse states that Rangini is the name of Srimati Radharani's pet doe.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which bird serves as the pet cakori of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text lists Carucnadrika as Srimati Radharani's pet cakori bird.

2.198.verse_text: Tundikeri is the name of Radharani's pet swan, who is fond of swimming in Radha-kunda. Madhuri is Radharani's pet elephant and Suksmadhi and Subha are Her two pet parrots.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of Srimati Radharani's pet swan who delights in swimming within the waters of Radha-kunda?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Tundikeri as the pet swan of Radharani, noting her fondness for swimming in Radha-kunda.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the pet elephant belonging to Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Madhuri as the pet elephant of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the names of Srimati Radharani's two pet parrots?
questions.explanation: The text states that Suksmadhi and Subha are the names of the two pet parrots belonging to Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what specific activity does the pet swan Tundikeri enjoy performing?
questions.explanation: The text describes Tundikeri the swan as being fond of swimming in Radha-kunda.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, if Tundikeri is the pet swan of Srimati Radharani, which animal is identified as Her pet elephant?
questions.explanation: The verse confirms that while Tundikeri is the swan, Madhuri is the specific pet elephant of Srimati Radharani.

2.199.verse_text: The two parrots perfectly imitate Lalita-devi's playful jokes spoken to her master and mistress (Sri Sri Radha-Krsna). By this wonderful repetition the parrots astonish the gopis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the unique behavior of the two pet parrots of Srimati Radharani that astonishes the gopis?
options: They perfectly imitate the playful jokes spoken by Lalita-devi to Radha and Krishna
They recite the names of the gopis in a sweet, rhythmic chanting
They sing the glories of the Divine Couple in the modes of different ragas
They fly to the forest to fetch garlands for the Divine Couple
questions.correct_answer: They perfectly imitate the playful jokes spoken by Lalita-devi to Radha and Krishna
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the two parrots, Suksmadhi and Subha, astonish the gopis by perfectly repeating the playful jokes Lalita-devi tells to her master and mistress.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the names of the two pet parrots belonging to Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Suksmadhi and Subha as the names of the two parrots belonging to Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which associate of Srimati Radharani is known for speaking the playful jokes that the parrots mimic?
questions.explanation: The parrots are described as repeating the jokes spoken specifically by Lalita-devi to Radha and Krishna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of Srimati Radharani's pet swan that swims in Radha-kunda?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Tundikeri as the pet swan of Radharani who enjoys swimming in Radha-kunda.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which pet of Srimati Radharani is described as an elephant?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Madhuri as Radharani's pet elephant.

2.200.verse_text: Srimati Radharani's Ornaments: Srimati Radharani's tilaka marking is named Smarayantra. Her jeweled necklace is named Harimohana, Her jeweled earrings are named Rocana and the pearl decorating Her nose is named Prabhakari.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific name of the tilaka marking worn by Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Srimati Radharani's tilaka marking as Smarayantra.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, by what name is the jeweled necklace of Srimati Radharani known?
questions.explanation: Srimati Radharani's jeweled necklace is identified in the text as Harimohana.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the names of the jeweled earrings worn by the Queen of Vrindavana, Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text states that Srimati Radharani's jeweled earrings are named Rocana.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name given to the pearl that decorates the nose of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text describes the pearl decorating Srimati Radharani's nose as being named Prabhakari.

2.201.verse_text: Her locket which contains a picture of Lord Krsna, is named Madana. Her Syamantaka jewel is also known as Sankhacuda-siromani (Sankhacuda's crest-jewel).

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the locket Srimati Radharani wears that contains a beautiful picture of Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Srimati Radharani's locket, which contains a picture of Lord Krsna, is named Madana.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, by what other name is Srimati Radharani's Syamantaka jewel known?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the Syamantaka jewel as being known as Sankhacuda-siromani, meaning Sankhacuda's crest-jewel.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific designation given to the jewel that serves as a crest for Srimati Radharani, referencing the demon Sankhacuda?
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that the Syamantaka jewel worn by Her is named Sankhacuda-siromani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the significance of the name Sankhacuda-siromani in relation to Srimati Radharani's jewels?
options: It refers to the crest-jewel of the demon Sankhacuda
It describes the sound of Her ankle bells
It represents the locket holding Lord Krsna's picture
It identifies the pearl on Her nose
questions.correct_answer: It refers to the crest-jewel of the demon Sankhacuda
questions.explanation: The text explains that the Syamantaka jewel is also known as Sankhacuda-siromani, which translates to Sankhacuda's crest-jewel.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what item worn by Srimati Radharani is described as featuring a portrait of Her beloved Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text identifies that Her locket is the specific ornament that contains a picture of Lord Krsna.

2.202.verse_text: The auspicious jewel She wears around Her neck is called Puspavan because it eclipses the simultaneous rising of the sun and moon (puspavan) with its splendor. Her anklets are called Catakarava because their tinkling sounds resemble the warbling of cataka birds. Her bracelets are called Manikarvura.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the auspicious jewel that Srimati Radharani wears around Her neck, known for eclipsing the splendor of the sun and moon?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the jewel around Her neck is called Puspavan because its splendor eclipses the simultaneous rising of the sun and moon.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are Srimati Radharani's tinkling anklets called, which sound like the warbling of cataka birds?
questions.explanation: The text describes Her anklets as Catakarava, noting that their sounds resemble the warbling of cataka birds.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, by what name are the bracelets worn by Srimati Radharani known?
questions.explanation: The verse specifically identifies Her bracelets as Manikarvura.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the jewel named Puspavan is unique because it is said to do what?
options: Eclipse the simultaneous rising of the sun and moon
Stun Lord Krsna with its brilliant shine
Contain a miniature portrait of Lord Krsna
questions.correct_answer: Eclipse the simultaneous rising of the sun and moon
questions.explanation: The text states the jewel is named Puspavan because it eclipses the simultaneous rising of the sun and moon with its splendor.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the tinkling sound of which of Srimati Radharani's ornaments is compared to the chirping of cataka birds?
questions.explanation: The text describes that Her anklets are called Catakarava because their tinkling sounds resemble the warbling of cataka birds.

2.203.verse_text: Srimati Radharani's signet ring is named Vipaksamardini. Her sash is named Kancanacitrangi and Her ankle-bells, which stun Lord Krsna with their tinkling sounds, are named Ratnagopura.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of Srimati Radharani's signet ring?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Srimati Radharani's signet ring is named Vipaksamardini.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name given to Srimati Radharani's beautiful sash?
questions.explanation: Srimati Radharani's sash is referred to by the name Kancanacitrangi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the ankle-bells worn by Srimati Radharani that possess the power to stun Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Srimati Radharani's ankle-bells as Ratnagopura and notes their ability to stun Lord Krsna with their tinkling sounds.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what specific effect do the tinkling sounds of Radharani's ankle-bells, Ratnagopura, have upon Lord Krsna?
options: They remind Him of the forest
They cause Him to fall asleep
questions.explanation: The description of the Ratnagopura ankle-bells explicitly mentions that their tinkling sounds stun Lord Krsna.

2.204.verse_text: Srimati Radharani's garments are named Meghambara. Her upper garment is red like a ruby and it is the favorite of Lord Hari. Radharani's lower garment is the color of a blue cloud and it is Her own favorite.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific name given to the garments worn by Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Srimati Radharani's garments are named Meghambara.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the distinct color of Srimati Radharani's upper garment?
questions.explanation: The text describes Radharani's upper garment as being red like a ruby and noted as the favorite of Lord Hari.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which color describes Srimati Radharani's lower garment, which is also identified as Her own personal favorite?
options: The color of a white lotus
The color of a blue cloud
The color of a blazing fire
The color of a golden champaka flower
questions.correct_answer: The color of a blue cloud
questions.explanation: The text states that Radharani's lower garment is the color of a blue cloud and is noted as Her favorite.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, for whom is the red, ruby-colored upper garment of Srimati Radharani considered a favorite?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that Srimati Radharani's ruby-red upper garment is the favorite of Lord Hari (Krsna).

2.205.verse_text: Srimati Radhrani's jeweled mirror is named Sudhamsudarpaharana, which means "that which removes (harana) the moon's (sudhamsu) pride (darpa)."

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the beautiful name of Srimati Radharani's jeweled mirror?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Srimati Radharani's jeweled mirror as Sudhamsudarpaharana.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the symbolic meaning behind the name of Srimati Radharani's mirror, Sudhamsudarpaharana?
options: That which captures the moon's light
That which removes the moon's pride
That which reflects the beauty of Vrindavana
That which brings joy to the gopis
questions.correct_answer: That which removes the moon's pride
questions.explanation: The name Sudhamsudarpaharana translates to 'that which removes (harana) the moon's (sudhamsu) pride (darpa).'

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the golden stick used by Srimati Radharani for applying mascara?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the golden mascara stick belonging to Srimati Radharani as Narmada.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the jeweled comb that Srimati Radharani uses?
questions.explanation: The text clearly states that Srimati Radharani's jeweled comb is named Svastida.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of Srimati Radharani's private flower garden?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Srimati Radharani's private flower garden as Kandarpakuhali.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of the upper garment worn by Srimati Radharani, which is a favorite of Lord Hari?
questions.explanation: The text states that Srimati Radharani's upper garment is red like a ruby and is a favorite of Lord Hari.

2.206.verse_text: Her golden stick for applying mascara is named Narmada, Her jeweled comb is named Svastida and Her private flower garden is named Kandarpakuhali.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of Srimati Radharani’s golden stick used for applying mascara?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Srimati Radharani's golden mascara stick as Narmada.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific name given to the jeweled comb of the Divine Queen, Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text states that Radharani's jeweled comb is known by the name Svastida.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, by what name is Srimati Radharani’s private flower garden known?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Srimati Radharani's private garden as Kandarpakuhali.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the significance of the name of Srimati Radharani's jeweled mirror, Sudhamsudarpaharana?
options: That which captures the beauty of the stars
That which removes the pride of the moon
That which reflects the radiance of Krishna
That which holds the nectar of the forest
questions.correct_answer: That which removes the pride of the moon
questions.explanation: The text explains the etymology of Sudhamsudarpaharana as that which removes the pride of the moon.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which object of Srimati Radharani is described as a 'vine of lightning'?
questions.explanation: The text describes the golden jasmine vine in Radharani's garden as Tadidvalli, meaning the vine of lightning.

2.207.verse_text: In Radharani's garden is a vine of golden jasmine flowers that She has named Tadidvalli ("the vine of lightning"). Her private lake bears Her own name (Radha-kunda) and on the shore of that lake is a kadamba tree that is the site of very confidential talks between Her and Lord Krsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what name has Srimati Radharani given to the vine of golden jasmine flowers found in Her garden?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that the vine of golden jasmine flowers in Radharani's garden is named Tadidvalli, which translates to 'the vine of lightning'.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the private lake that bears Srimati Radharani's own name?
questions.explanation: The scripture states that Her private lake is named after Her, which is Radha-kunda.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what variety of tree grows on the shore of Radha-kunda and serves as the site for the confidential talks of the Divine Couple?
questions.explanation: It is mentioned that a kadamba tree is situated on the shore of Radha-kunda, acting as the location for confidential pastimes.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the meaning or translation given for the name of the golden jasmine vine, Tadidvalli?
questions.explanation: The text describes Tadidvalli as 'the vine of lightning'.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, where do the most confidential talks between Sri Krsna and Srimati Radharani take place?
options: Under the shade of the kadamba tree on the shore of Radha-kunda
Within the private garden named Kandarpakuhali
Beside the bank of the Yamuna near the vine of lightning
Inside the house of Mother Yasoda
questions.correct_answer: Under the shade of the kadamba tree on the shore of Radha-kunda
questions.explanation: The text identifies the kadamba tree on the shore of Radha-kunda as the site for very confidential talks between Radha and Krsna.

2.208.verse_text: Her favorite ragas are mallara and dhanasri and Her favorite dances are chalikya and rudravallaki.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which musical ragas are cherished most dearly by Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies mallara and dhanasri as Her favorite ragas.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the specific names of the dance styles that delight Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Her favorite dances are chalikya and rudravallaki.

2.209.verse_text: Srimati Radharani's glorious birth occurred on the eighth day of the bright moon in the month of Bhadra. Although generally not full on that day, the moon appeared full to celebrate Radharani's appearance in this world.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, on which lunar day of the bright fortnight in the month of Bhadra did the glorious birth of Srimati Radharani take place?
questions.explanation: Srimati Radharani's appearance day is explicitly stated to be the eighth day of the bright moon in the month of Bhadra.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what celestial phenomenon occurred to honor the appearance of Srimati Radharani, even though it was not technically its natural time?
options: The sun stood still in the sky
A shower of celestial flowers fell
questions.explanation: The text notes that although the moon was generally not full on that day, it appeared full to celebrate Radharani's appearance.

2.210.verse_text: In this way we have revealed a little about the countless associates of Sri Sri Radha and Krsna, the two monarchs of Sri Vrndavana.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how are Sri Sri Radha and Krsna described in relation to the sacred realm of Vrndavana?
options: As the guardians of the gates
questions.explanation: The text explicitly refers to Sri Sri Radha and Krsna as the two monarchs of Sri Vrndavana.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what has the author accomplished by describing the various associates of the Divine Couple?
options: A complete catalog of every living being
A small revelation regarding their countless associates
A historical record of the cowherd dynasty
questions.correct_answer: A small revelation regarding their countless associates
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that the author has revealed only a little about the countless associates of Radha and Krsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, whom does the author identify as the central focus of the associates being described?
options: The associates of Sri Sri Radha and Krsna
The devotees of the spiritual world
questions.correct_answer: The associates of Sri Sri Radha and Krsna
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions the associates of Sri Sri Radha and Krsna as the primary subjects of the description.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what does the author acknowledge about the scope of his description of the associates?
options: He has provided a exhaustive list
He has revealed only a little
He has described every single personality
He has documented only the queens
questions.correct_answer: He has revealed only a little
questions.explanation: The text states the author has revealed 'a little' about the associates, acknowledging they are countless.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which location is explicitly mentioned as the domain of the two monarchs?
questions.explanation: The text designates Sri Vrndavana as the place where Radha and Krsna are the monarchs.
